title: Could Russia use fake separatists to destabilize Estonia and discredit NATO?
text:
In recent weeks, references to a pro-Russian separatist movement in neighboring Estonia have begun appearing with increasing frequency on social media. However, not everyone is convinced that the so-called "Narva People's Republic" is worthy of serious attention, writes James Rice.
The post Could Russia use fake separatists to destabilize Estonia and discredit NATO? appeared first on Atlantic Council.

title: Oil waivers risk sustaining Russia’s war effort amid the Iran war
text:
Relieving US sanctions on Russian oil would give Moscow a reprieve just as financial pressure was beginning to constrain its room for maneuver in its war against Ukraine. 
The post Oil waivers risk sustaining Russia’s war effort amid the Iran war appeared first on Atlantic Council.

title: Putin is counting on Western disunity to hand him victory in Ukraine
text:
Russian President Vladimir Putin has been unable to defeat the Ukrainians on the battlefield but he remains confident that Western disunity will ultimately hand him victory in Ukraine, writes Mykola Bielieskov.
The post Putin is counting on Western disunity to hand him victory in Ukraine appeared first on Atlantic Council.

title: Wartime Ukraine offers global lessons on the future of cyber resilience
text:
The twelve years of cyber warfare that have accompanied Russia’s escalating invasion of Ukraine have transformed the country’s digital environment into a proving ground for modern conflict, write Oleksandr Bakalynskyi and Maggie McDonough.
The post Wartime Ukraine offers global lessons on the future of cyber resilience appeared first on Atlantic Council.

title: The Iran war is good for the Russian economy but bad for Putin’s prestige
text:
From Armenia and Syria to Venezuela and Iran, Moscow’s inability since 2022 to aid its allies in times of crisis has seriously damaged Russia’s reputation as a global power, write Maksym Beznosiuk and Will Dixon.
The post The Iran war is good for the Russian economy but bad for Putin’s prestige appeared first on Atlantic Council.

title: The coming compute war in Ukraine
text:
For the United States, the coming compute war isn't just a Ukrainian problem—it's a preview of US challenges in future conflict. 
The post The coming compute war in Ukraine appeared first on Atlantic Council.

title: UN: Putin’s deportation of Ukrainian children is a crime against humanity
text:
Russia’s deportation of Ukrainian children is a crime against humanity, a new United Nations investigation has found. The mass abduction and indoctrination of Ukrainian children is part of a genocidal Kremlin plan to erase Ukrainian identity, writes Peter Dickinson. 
The post UN: Putin’s deportation of Ukrainian children is a crime against humanity appeared first on Atlantic Council.

title: Romania’s drone and energy plans with Ukraine make Europe stronger and more secure
text:
Romanian President Nicușor Dan hosted his Ukrainian counterpart on March 12, underscoring Bucharest’s growing role in regional security.
The post Romania’s drone and energy plans with Ukraine make Europe stronger and more secure appeared first on Atlantic Council.

title: Iran war highlights Ukraine’s rapid rise to drone superpower status
text:
The Iran war has sparked a sudden surge in international demand for Kyiv’s unique anti-drone expertise and highlighted Ukraine's rapid emergence as one of the world's leading drone warfare superpowers, writes David Kirichenko.
The post Iran war highlights Ukraine’s rapid rise to drone superpower status appeared first on Atlantic Council.

title: Energy under attack: What the Gulf can learn from Ukraine and Iraq
text:
In their successes and shortcomings, Iraq and Ukraine are cases worth studying by countries currently under attack from Iran.
The post Energy under attack: What the Gulf can learn from Ukraine and Iraq appeared first on Atlantic Council.

title: Friends-and-family diplomacy: Lessons for Europe from Gaza and Ukraine
text: Trump’s family and friends are trying—but failing—to broker peace in Gaza, Ukraine and beyond. The network they are building blends diplomacy with profit. Europe needs to find a way to shape the terms.
